data.content:
This is an **Android tombstone** from a .NET MAUI app using the **Mono runtime** (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`). Let me identify the runtime frames and attempt symbolication.

**Crash Summary:**
- **App**: `com.companyname.MauiTest`
- **Signal**: SIGABRT — Abort message: `"not compatible"`
- **Memory hint**: `"some intentional crash on startup."`
- **Runtime**: Mono (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`), BuildId: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`

**.NET Runtime Frames** (frames #01–#07 in `libmonosgen-2.0.so`):

| Frame | PC Offset |
|-------|-----------|
| #01 | `0x222098` |
| #02 | `0x2241ac` |
| #03 | `0x1abc80` |
| #04 | `0x1abd14` |
| #05 | `0x16dfa4` |
| #06 | `0x16db6c` |
| #07 | `0x16de04` |

Frame #08 is in anonymous memory — likely **JIT-compiled managed code**.

Let me try downloading debug symbols from the Microsoft symbol server using the ELF BuildId.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-935423b0e6e644dda97ff59a8d911213 && \
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be" && \
# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF debug symbols format
echo "=== Trying Microsoft Symbol Server ===" && \
# Format 1: direct .so with debug info
curl -sS -f -L -o libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  && echo "SUCCESS: Downloaded .so symbols" \
  || echo "FAILED: .so format" && \
# Format 2: _.debug with elf-buildid path
curl -sS -f -L -o libmonosgen-2.0.debug \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  && echo "SUCCESS: Downloaded _.debug symbols" \
  || echo "FAILED: _.debug format" && \
# Format 3: symstore ELF format (uppercase buildid, no ext)
curl -sS -f -L -o libmonosgen-2.0.sym \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  && echo "SUCCESS: Downloaded sym format" \
  || echo "FAILED: sym format"
arguments.description: Download Mono debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download Mono debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
This is an Android tombstone/crash log, not an Apple .ips crash log. The apple-crash-symbolication skill is specifically for Apple platforms (iOS, tvOS, Mac Catalyst, macOS), so it won't help here.

Let me analyze this crash log:

1. **Platform**: Android (arm64)
2. **App**: com.companyname.MauiTest (a .NET MAUI app)
3. **Signal**: SIGABRT (signal 6) - abort
4. **Abort message**: 'not compatible'
5. **Runtime**: Mono (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`) - this is the Mono runtime used by .NET MAUI on Android


6. **Crash frames**: The stack trace shows frames in `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`) and anonymous memory from JIT-compiled code, with the abort message indicating this was an intentional crash on startup.

To symbolicate the Mono runtime frames, I'd need the debug symbols matching that specific BuildId of the Mono runtime.

For Android ELF binaries, the approach differs from Apple—I'd use `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer` with ELF debug symbols rather than dSYMs. Microsoft publishes symbols for the .NET runtime, so I can check their symbol server to retrieve the matching debug information for this build.

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-935423b0e6e644dda97ff59a8d911213 && \
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be" && \
# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF debug symbols format
echo "=== Trying Microsoft Symbol Server ===" && \
# Format 1: direct .so with debug info
curl -sS -f -L -o libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  && echo "SUCCESS: Downloaded .so symbols" \
  || echo "FAILED: .so format" && \
# Format 2: _.debug with elf-buildid path
curl -sS -f -L -o libmonosgen-2.0.debug \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  && echo "SUCCESS: Downloaded _.debug symbols" \
  || echo "FAILED: _.debug format" && \
# Format 3: symstore ELF format (uppercase buildid, no ext)
curl -sS -f -L -o libmonosgen-2.0.sym \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  && echo "SUCCESS: Downloaded sym format" \
  || echo "FAILED: sym format"
arguments.description: Download Mono debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

  && echo "SUCCESS: Downloaded sym format" \
  || echo "FAILED: sym format"", "description": "Download Mono debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server", "initial_wait": 30}

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-935423b0e6e644dda97ff59a8d911213 && \
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be" && \
# Try additional symbol server URL patterns
# Pattern: NuGet symbol server
curl -sS -f -L -o mono.sym1 \
  "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}FFFFFFFF/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  && echo "SUCCESS: NuGet symbols" || echo "FAILED: NuGet" && \
# Pattern: Microsoft with elf-buildid-sym prefix
curl -sS -f -L -o mono.sym2 \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" \
  && echo "SUCCESS: .dbg format" || echo "FAILED: .dbg format" && \
# Check what tools we have
echo "=== Available tools ===" && \
which addr2line 2>/dev/null && echo "addr2line: available" || echo "addr2line: not available" && \
which llvm-symbolizer 2>/dev/null && echo "llvm-symbolizer: available" || echo "llvm-symbolizer: not available" && \
which dotnet 2>/dev/null && echo "dotnet: available" || echo "dotnet: not available" && \
# Clean up failed downloads
rm -f libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg libmonosgen-2.0.debug libmonosgen-2.0.sym mono.sym1 mono.sym2 2>/dev/null; true
arguments.description: Try additional symbol server patterns and check tools

data.reasoningText:
The Microsoft symbol server doesn't have the symbols for this specific build. Let me try a few more URL formats, and also try NuGet symbol server formats.

Actually, this BuildId might be from a pre-release or custom build. Let me also check if `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer` is available, and try additional symbol server URL patterns.

Let me also try the dotnet symbol tool (dotnet-symbol).
